paper_title: Biologically Inspired Climbing with a Hexapedal Robot
source_file: 机理文献/核心文献/22_Biologically inspired climbing with a hexapedal robot.pdf

render_review.all_pages: All 20 PDF pages were rendered to PNG at 150 dpi and visually checked in a single contact sheet. The double-column reading order, section transitions, figures, equations and Table I were also checked against per-page text extraction.
render_review.notes: Retained equations, thresholds and Table I values were read from individual page renders. The three retained image files were cropped from 260 dpi renders; all axes, legends, labels and captions needed for interpretation remain visible. Poppler emitted missing-display-font warnings for Symbol/ArialUnicode during rendering, but visual review found the retained mathematical symbols and labels readable.

sections_checked: Section 3 Spiny Feet for Climbing, Sections 3.1-3.2
Acknowledgments and full references checked for document completeness only

equations_checked.notes: Per-leg climbing power sizing P_l = mgv/(eta N_l) was visually checked. It sizes robot actuation and does not constrain the requested spine contact, array load sharing or paired-claw mechanism.

The periodic gait embedding and parallel/sequential decomposition were inspected. They are controller architecture notation; the transferable attachment-state sequence is retained through Fig. 13 instead.
Retained as E1. The average traction a_t, summation over f_t,i, divisor n, and n <= 6 condition were visually checked.

Retained as E2. b_t,i = k_p(a_t - f_t,i) was visually checked. The paper does not report the units or numerical value of b_t,i/k_p, so the evidence card explicitly marks them as unspecified and requiring calibration.

The relation g_i(phi_p) - g_i(phi_d) was inspected. It maintains joint-command continuity during the robot-specific recovery maneuver and is not required to preserve the transferable retry-state mechanism.

tables_checked.notes: All six controller rows and SC, LC, SF, velocity and distance-to-failure columns were visually checked. The evidence card retains the exact 960, 293 and 78.7 cm comparison and the 35.1-81.1 cm range for the remaining schemes. The unequal trial counts and Load Count definition are retained as warnings.

figures_checked.reason: Robot overview and foot location were inspected; the photograph does not add a transferable contact or load-sharing definition.
Modular body and wing range-of-motion images support body morphology but are adequately represented by the force/moment and redundancy notes.
Four-bar leg and crank foot path were inspected; Fig. 13 provides the more direct, labeled attachment trajectory used in the evidence card.
Opposed shock absorbers and extension/compression roles were inspected. The essential low/high radial-compliance tradeoff is retained textually; no numerical spring data are present.
Two-axis strain-gauge directions were inspected; the sensor accuracies and force directions are sufficiently captured in text and the parameter table.
Tree, brick and ground photographs are qualitative surface demonstrations and do not define roughness, contact geometry or a reusable parameter.
Biological inspiration photographs were inspected; the directional-spine concept is captured textually and does not require the image.
Irreplaceable joint definition of the soft/hard polyurethane toe regions, rows of spines, and the roll/pitch/yaw compliance hardware.
Generic gait decomposition and PD tracking diagram were inspected; it is not needed for the retained mechanism after Eqs. (2)-(4) are omitted.
Leg workspace was inspected. Fig. 13 contains the same workspace with the actual four-stage attachment trajectory and is the more compact sufficient evidence.
Required to preserve the four-stage presentation, drag-to-engage, stance/load and reverse/unload trajectory in joint and body coordinates.
Five-of-six wave-gait timing was visually checked. The exact 5/6 duty factor and 1/6 phase offset are more compactly retained as parameters.
Directly links measured foot traction, average traction/deadband and the speed-up/slow-down controller state that implements Eqs. (5)-(6).
The pawing shortcut from failed attachment to early lift/retry was inspected; the state transition is compactly and unambiguously retained in text.
Integrated control block diagram was inspected. It is controller architecture rather than a needed contact/load-sharing geometry.
Open-loop and full-feedback force profiles were visually compared. The paper supplies no raw dataset; the smoother/tighter-bounds conclusion is preserved as trend evidence.
The approximately 12 m building climb photograph is a system demonstration; the distance and speed limitation are fully retained textually.

omitted_sections: General wall-climbing background and literature review
Electronics, communications, processor and battery implementation not affecting contact/load sharing
Motor power and gearbox sizing details
Generic oscillator/gait notation beyond the retained attachment-state sequence
Turning-control implementation not required for grasp-force or failure-recovery modeling

uncertain_items.detail: The 25 and 15 micrometer tip radii and the stated brick/stone/cinder-block performance were verified, but no topography, friction, strength, sample count or success probability is reported. The relationship is retained only as a scale/trend observation.
The paper states 'a few newtons at most' without a table, test setup, material or dispersion. It is retained only as a qualitative order-of-magnitude upper bound.
Material Shore hardness and directional qualitative stiffness are given, but no effective stiffness, travel, damping or toe geometry-to-stiffness relation is reported.
The equation and direction of action are clear, but k_p, b_t,i units, sampling interval and stability limits are absent.

Most configurations used three successive 1 m climbs, whereas full feedback used ten runs accumulated to failure. Distance-to-failure comparisons are retained as reported trends, not equal-sample inferential statistics.
In this paper the term is a signed normal pull-in force at the foot, not an adhesive material force. The route card explicitly prevents that terminology from being transferred incorrectly.

known_warnings: Foot-level contact thresholds and recovery logic must not be treated as single-spine thresholds or probabilities.
The robot-level average traction controller is not a complete paired-claw force/moment balance and ignores heterogeneous contact capacity.
Shore hardness values do not directly supply equivalent toe, ankle or leg spring constants.
Surface-class observations do not provide a numerical roughness-to-capacity law.
Table I compares unequal sample counts and does not report confidence intervals or raw runs.
The long building climb validates integrated system feasibility but not controlled failure transfer, 6D wrench capacity or red-brick single-contact mechanics.
Poppler emitted missing Symbol/ArialUnicode display-font warnings; retained symbols and labels were visually checked and found legible.
character_count_method: Count of CJK Unified Ideographs in evidence_card.md; within the allowed 3500-character extension because the paper contains separate single-foot attachment, hierarchical compliance, active inter-foot load-sharing and whole-robot validation evidence.
